pr.title: renamed, documented and slightly refactored lbvector functions
pr.author: Ka-lua

summary: Renamed and documented many lbvector helpers, updated their declarations, and propagated symbol-name changes through C and assembly callsites across camera, fighter, item, stage, collision, and library files. The PR mattered because it converted anonymous func_8000xxxx vector utilities into reusable math names while preserving traceability to original addresses and keeping uncertain behavior names conservative after review.
what_changed: Renamed basic vector operations from lower-case or anonymous names to lbvector_* names, including lbvector_Normalize, lbvector_NormalizeXY, lbvector_Add, lbvector_Add_xy, lbvector_Sub, lbvector_Diff, and lbvector_CrossprodNormalized.

Renamed previously anonymous vector/math functions such as func_8000D620 to lbvector_Angle, func_8000D790 to lbvector_AngleXY, func_8000D8F4 to lbvector_RotateAboutUnitAxis, func_8000DC6C to lbvector_Mirror, func_8000DDAC to lbvector_Lerp, func_8000DF0C to lbvector_EulerAnglesFromONB, func_8000DFF4 to lbvector_EulerAnglesFromPartialONB, func_8000E138 to lbvector_ApplyEulerRotation, func_8000E210 to lbvector_WorldToScreen, and func_8000E530 to lbvector_CreateEulerMatrix.
Left at least one uncertain function with an address-derived name, lbvector_8000E838, matching review guidance that the proposed camera-specific name could be misleading.
Updated src/melee/lb/lbvector.h prototypes to match the new names and parameter naming, including result_angles, angles, axis, and result-style parameters.

Propagated renamed symbols through many assembly files, changing branch-link call operands such as lbvector_diff to lbvector_Diff, lbvector_normalize to lbvector_Normalize, lbvector_rotate to lbvector_Rotate, func_8000D8F4 to lbvector_RotateAboutUnitAxis, and func_8000D790 to lbvector_AngleXY.

Added explanatory comments for math behavior, including acos/asin/atan2 extern hints, angle-between-vector functions, unit-axis rotation, mirror/reflection behavior, lerp behavior, Euler angle extraction, world-to-screen projection, and Euler matrix construction.
Performed small local refactors in lbvector.c for readability, such as renaming variables from generic f1/f5/c to cosine, lena_lenb, result, axis, angles, and result_angles.
Kept original function addresses in comments for renamed functions, preserving a useful mapping from func_8000xxxx symbols to semantic names.
smart_moves: Renaming all occurrences in .s, .c, and .h files avoided a half-renamed state where C prototypes and assembly callsites disagree.
Review converged on short, subsystem-neutral names, which is important for library functions used outside their most obvious caller.
Using comments like "8000D8F4" beside the new semantic name preserved address searchability for decomp work and future cross-references.
Documenting mathematical preconditions, such as the unit-length axis requirement for lbvector_RotateAboutUnitAxis, turns a matching helper into a safer API for later decompilation.
Parameter renames to result, axis, unit_mirror_axis, angles, and result_angles made function intent clearer without requiring structural changes.
Clamping cosine to [-1, 1] in the angle functions was already present behavior, but the rename and comments made the numerical intent explicit.
decomp_lessons: When renaming a library helper used from assembly, update every asm callsite as well as the source implementation and header prototypes.
Prefer names that describe general math behavior over names inferred from a single caller; a camera callsite does not necessarily make the function camera-specific.
If a function's behavior is still uncertain, an address-preserving name such as lbvector_8000E838 is better than a misleading descriptive name.
Keep original addresses in comments around newly named functions so old func_8000xxxx references remain searchable.
Small readability refactors are useful when they expose the math formula, but should be conservative in matching-sensitive code.
Document hidden preconditions such as unit vectors, Euler rotation order, and radians-vs-degrees assumptions as soon as they are understood.
For math helpers, names like AngleXY, NormalizeXY, CrossprodNormalized, Lerp, Mirror, and WorldToScreen encode dimensionality or coordinate-space assumptions that later callers can reuse.
naming_conventions: Adopt lbvector_ prefix for vector-library helpers.
Use short CamelCase operation names after the prefix, e.g. lbvector_Normalize, lbvector_Rotate, lbvector_WorldToScreen, and lbvector_AngleXY.
Use XY suffix for 2D-on-XY-plane variants, as in lbvector_NormalizeXY and lbvector_AngleXY.
Avoid overly long snake_case names such as lbvector_project_world_to_screen_coord when a shorter lbvector_WorldToScreen-style name communicates the same behavior.
Avoid caller-specific names such as lbvector_some_camera_vector_computation unless the function is proven to be camera-only.
Address-derived fallback names like lbvector_8000E838 are acceptable for uncertain semantics.
Parameter names should communicate roles: result, result_angles, axis, unit_mirror_axis, angles, cobj, pos3d, and screenCoords.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Most assembly changes were symbol-only callsite renames, preserving the same branch targets/opcodes while replacing anonymous or old lbvector names.
The diff shows broad asm propagation in files such as asm/melee/cm/camera.s, asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s, item asm, ground/stage asm, lb asm, and collision asm.
C source retained matching-sensitive details such as the local dummy function used to force float order in .sdata2.
The sqrtf_accurate helper still uses __frsqrte iterations; formatting changed, but the algorithm and extra iteration comment remained.
Cross-references to PSVECCrossProduct remained intact while wrapping the result in lbvector_CrossprodNormalized and lbvector_Normalize.
Original address comments next to renamed implementations provide a practical grep bridge between old assembly symbols and new names.
review_feedback: PsiLupan requested shorter naming and suggested lbvector_WorldToScreen instead of lbvector_project_world_to_screen_coord.
PsiLupan warned that lbvector_some_camera_vector_computation was not necessarily more descriptive and could be misleading if the function is used elsewhere; suggested lbvector_8000E838.
PsiLupan suggested lbvector_AngleXY for the XY-plane angle helper rather than the lower-case lbvector_angle_xy.
The author initially noted they could not build the project and asked someone to check whether it compiled.
The author later commented that the PR did not OK yet, that their build setup worked after downgrading to devkitPPC r39, and that they would update with a working version.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbvector_8000E838 once its behavior is understood.
Search for remaining func_8000Dxxx or func_8000Exxx vector helpers that still lack lbvector names.
Check whether lbvector_Add_xy casing was later normalized to lbvector_AddXY for consistency with NormalizeXY and AngleXY.
Search review history for project-wide conventions on lbvector CamelCase versus snake_case naming.
Look for later decompilation of camera world-to-screen callsites using lbvector_WorldToScreen.
